rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 29, 2025, Line 1. RESPONDENT BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss. Respondent Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Dismiss is GRANTED. Respondent moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 583.250. Good cause appearing. The matter is ordered dismissed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $4223.00 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment As To Claimant Brandon Jackson-Knight
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 29, 2025, Line 2. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Default Judgment As To Claimant Brandon Jackson-Knight. Petitioner The People of the State of California's unopposed Motion For Default Judgment As To Claimant Brandon Jackson-Knight is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Petitioner moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 437(c) "on the grounds that there are no triable issues of material fact and that there is no defense to the action." There is no section 437, subdivision ©, so the reference must be to section 437c, the statute governing motions for summary judgment and summary adjudication. This conclusion is confirmed by the quoted text. Which sets out the standard for a motion for summary judgment by a petitioner or plaintiff. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivisions (a)(1) and (c).) Such motions must be filed at least 81 days before the hearing date. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivisions (a)(2).) The court cannot shorten he notice period. This motion was filed less than 60 days before the hearing date. Further, the moving part must submit a separate statement. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivisions (b)(2).) Courts rely heavily on separate statements. Petitioner here did not file a separate statement. The authority identified in the notice of motion controls. For defaults and default judgments, the court draws Petitioner's attention to Code of Civil Procedure section 585 et seq and SFLR 8.8. A motion noticed under section 585 and SFLR 8.8 may be filed and served consistent with Code of Civil Procedure section 1005, subdivisions (b). Petitioner is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on April 29, 2025. Petitioner shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 29, 2025, Line 11. PLAINTIFF HARRY FISCHER's Motion To Compel Depositions Of The Santa Barbara Auto Group Employees. Plaintiff Harry Fischer Jr.'s unopposed Motion To Compel Depositions Of The Santa Barbara Auto Group Employees is, based on moving party's notice that the motion is withdrawn, OFF CALENDAR. Moving Party is advised that stacked discovery motions are disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 29, 2025, Line 9. DEFENDANTs THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA A PUBLIC CORPORATION ,and SHREYA PATEL, M.D. MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants The Regents of the University of California and Shreya Patel, M.D.'s Motion to Strike Amended Complaint is GRANTED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
